description: Improve the side panel's tab state visibility by adding a small colored status dot to the PluginIcon and showing the TabStateHint on the collapsed accordion header, so users can see plugin readiness at a glance without expanding cards.

userStories.title: Add a status dot indicator to PluginIcon based on tab state
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a small colored dot on each plugin icon that clearly distinguishes between ready (green), unavailable (amber/yellow), and closed (no dot / muted) states so I can tell at a glance which plugins are active.
acceptanceCriteria: PluginIcon accepts a new optional 'tabState' prop of type 'ready' | 'unavailable' | 'closed'
When tabState is 'ready', a small filled circle in the success color (bg-success, which is the green theme token) appears at the bottom-right corner of the icon, overlapping the border slightly
When tabState is 'unavailable', a small filled circle in the primary color (bg-primary, the yellow theme token) appears at the same position
When tabState is 'closed', no dot is shown
The dot has a 2px ring of bg-card color to separate it from the icon border, consistent with badge-over-avatar patterns
The icon opacity treatment is REMOVED — ready/non-ready no longer uses opacity-50. The status dot is now the sole indicator
The dot size is proportional to the icon size (roughly size * 0.3 diameter, minimum 8px)
PluginCard passes plugin.tabState to PluginIcon instead of the boolean ready prop
All Storybook stories in PluginCard.stories.tsx and PluginList.stories.tsx are updated to pass tabState
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginIcon.tsx

Current behavior: The `ready` boolean prop controls `opacity-50` on lines 49 and 65. Replace this with a richer `tabState` prop.

Design approach: Position the dot using `absolute` within a `relative` container. The icon container div already has explicit width/height via style prop. Add position: relative to the outer div, then add an absolutely-positioned dot div at bottom-right.

Implementation:
1. Change the `ready: boolean` prop to `tabState?: 'ready' | 'unavailable' | 'closed'` (keep backward compatible — default to 'closed' if undefined)
2. Remove the `opacity-50` class from both SVG and letter avatar variants
3. Instead, use the inactive SVG (`iconInactiveSvg`) when tabState is NOT 'ready', and the active SVG when tabState IS 'ready' (this preserves the current icon-swapping behavior)
4. Add the dot as a child of the outer div:
```tsx
{(tabState === 'ready' || tabState === 'unavailable') && (
  <div
    className={`absolute border-2 border-card rounded-full ${
      tabState === 'ready' ? 'bg-success' : 'bg-primary'
    }`}
    style={{
      width: Math.max(8, Math.round(size * 0.3)),
      height: Math.max(8, Math.round(size * 0.3)),
      bottom: -2,
      right: -2,
    }}
  />
)}
```
5. Add `relative` to the outer div className

File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.tsx
Change line 120 from `ready={plugin.tabState === 'ready'}` to `tabState={plugin.tabState}`.

Update all Storybook stories that use PluginIcon or pass the ready prop.

userStories.title: Show tab state hint text on the collapsed plugin card header
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a brief status hint next to the plugin name (like 'Open slack.com' or 'Waiting...') without expanding the accordion, so that I immediately know what action to take.
acceptanceCriteria: A compact inline status hint appears next to the plugin display name in the accordion trigger (collapsed header), not just inside the expanded content
When tabState is 'closed', a small text in text-destructive says 'Open {domain}' (or 'No tab' if no domain extractable) — visible in the collapsed header
When tabState is 'unavailable', a small text in text-muted-foreground says 'Waiting...' — visible in the collapsed header
When tabState is 'ready', no inline hint is shown (clean header)
The existing TabStateHint inside the expanded accordion content is REMOVED to avoid duplication
The hint text uses text-[10px] size and is truncated if it overflows the available header space
The hint sits below the plugin displayName within the same flex column, not on a separate row
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes

userStories.title: Update E2E tests for new tab state visual indicators
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests to verify the new tab state dot and inline hint so that visual regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E tests in e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts that check for tab state visual changes are updated to look for the new status dot indicator instead of the old opacity-based check
E2E tests verify that the inline hint text ('Open {domain}' / 'Waiting...') appears in the collapsed header
E2E tests verify that the hint disappears when tab state changes to 'ready'
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/side-panel-data-flow.e2e.ts

Existing tests check for tab state changes (around lines 95-200). These tests likely look for opacity changes or text content inside the expanded accordion. Update them to:
1. Check for the status dot element (look for a small div with bg-success or bg-primary class)
2. Check for the inline hint text in the collapsed header
3. Verify transitions: closed (hint visible, no green dot) → ready (no hint, green dot) → closed (hint returns, dot disappears)

The tests should NOT expand the accordion to find the hint — the whole point is that the hint is visible in the collapsed state.

Also check e2e/tab-state-sync.e2e.ts and e2e/onboarding.e2e.ts for any tab-state-related assertions that need updating.
